pr_summary: This PR adds new functionality to the codebase.
verdict_reason: The review found critical-severity issues requiring attention.

findings.title: Infinite Loop with No Timeout
findings.description: The while loop in the pollOrderStatus function will continue indefinitely if the order status remains 'pending'. This can cause the service to hang and consume excessive resources.
findings.suggestion: Add a timeout or a maximum number of retries to the loop to prevent it from running indefinitely.

findings.title: INSECURE DIRECT OBJECT REFERENCE (IDOR) in Order Cancellation
findings.description: The cancelOrder function does not verify if the requesting user owns the order being cancelled, allowing an attacker to cancel any order. This is a high-severity issue as it allows unauthorized access to sensitive data.
findings.suggestion: Add a check to ensure the order belongs to the requesting user, e.g., `if (order.userId !== userId) { throw new Error('Unauthorized'); }`

findings.title: NULL DEREFERENCE / UNHANDLED NULLS
findings.description: The order.userEmail is not checked for null before passing it to the notification service, which can cause a null pointer exception if the email is not set.
findings.suggestion: Add a null check: if (!order.userEmail) { throw new Error(`User email not found for order: ${orderId}`); }

findings.title: INSECURE DIRECT OBJECT REFERENCE (IDOR) in Order Status Polling
findings.description: The pollOrderStatus function does not verify if the requesting user owns the order being polled, allowing an attacker to access sensitive data.
findings.suggestion: Add a check to ensure the order belongs to the requesting user, e.g., `if (order.userId !== userId) { throw new Error('Unauthorized'); }`

findings.title: Sequential Async Operations
findings.description: The bulkCancelOrders function cancels orders sequentially, which can lead to performance issues when dealing with a large number of orders. This can cause the service to slow down and become unresponsive.
findings.suggestion: Use Promise.all() or a similar mechanism to cancel orders in parallel, improving the overall performance of the function.

findings.line_content: export async function bulkCancelOrders(orderIds: string[]): Promise<void> {
findings.title: MISSING INPUT VALIDATION
findings.description: The function does not check if the orderIds array is empty or null, which can cause incorrect results.
findings.suggestion: Add a check: if (!orderIds || orderIds.length === 0) { throw new Error('No order IDs provided'); }

positive_observations: The code follows a consistent structure.
Function naming is clear and descriptive.
